Why CSS overflow: scroll Shows No Scrollbar on macOS Chrome (vs. Windows)

You set overflow: scroll, then realize Mac Chrome shows no scrollbar — but your coworker on Windows sees one fine. This post starts with the four overflow values, then explains macOS's Overlay Scrollbar mechanism — why Chrome on macOS doesn't always show a scrollbar even with overflow: scroll. Three fixes: ::-webkit-scrollbar to force it, the newer standard scrollbar-width and scrollbar-color, and flipping macOS's "Show scroll bars" system setting to "Always".
The Browser Render Pipeline Every Frontend Engineer Should Know

A student asked me about this recently, and I think it's actually important stuff for frontend engineers. We're constantly working with browsers — not knowing how they work seems like a missed opportunity.
How to Stop Users From Selecting Specific Elements
Sometimes you want a specific section on your site to be unselectable by the user. Here are a few ways to do that.
Can I Use? A Five-Minute Guide to Checking CSS / HTML / JS Browser Support

Frontend devs use new CSS / HTML / JS features all the time — sometimes without realizing they're not supported in every browser. Can I Use is the go-to support-checking service. This post is a five-minute tour of using it — searching for features, reading the support matrix and color legend, the vendor-prefix tooling, and a quick aside on what CSS prefixes are and when they matter.

The 7-1 pattern is about organising your directory structure. Back when you had to dump all your CSS in one place, maintenance was painful. Sass lets you use `@import` plus folder organisation — that's the 7-1 pattern.
BEM is a CSS design pattern that makes class architecture easier to maintain. The main downside is overly long class names — but plenty of real projects still use it.
OOCSS is another CSS design pattern. The most prominent framework using it is Bootstrap.
CSS design patterns are concepts, conventions, and ways of thinking that improve dev and maintenance efficiency. Let's start with SMACSS.
Drawing a Heart in Pure CSS
Hit a CSS thing I didn't know while working on my "Vue Out a Girlfriend" practice project. Quick notes on how to draw a heart in pure CSS.
Using CSS to Detect Layout Breakage
When slicing layouts in CSS, you'll often hit layout breakage. Browsers don't natively show grid lines, so here's a CSS trick to enable one.
